__data__.text:
Prodapt - 5G Network FAQ

Q: What is 5G and how is it different from 4G LTE?
A: 5G (Fifth Generation) is our newest wireless technology offering significantly faster
download speeds (up to 1 Gbps in ideal conditions), lower latency (as low as 10ms), and
better support for many connected devices. 4G LTE typically delivers 25-100 Mbps with
40-60ms latency. 5G uses both mid-band (C-band) and mmWave spectrum depending on your location.

Q: Do I need a new phone for 5G?
A: Yes. Your device must support 5G NR bands n77 and n260 used by Prodapt. Compatible
devices include iPhone 12 and newer, Samsung Galaxy S21 and newer, Google Pixel 6 and newer.
Check compatibility at prodapt.com/5g-devices.

Q: Why does my 5G icon show but speeds feel slow?
A: The 5G icon indicates connection to a 5G cell, but speed depends on:
- Distance from tower and line-of-sight (mmWave requires near-tower proximity)
- Network congestion during peak hours (6-10 PM)
- Your plan's speed tier (Standard Unlimited caps 5G at 250 Mbps; Unlimited Plus is uncapped)
- Indoor penetration (5G mid-band penetrates buildings better than mmWave)

Q: What areas have 5G coverage?
A: Prodapt 5G covers 280+ metro areas. Rural 5G expansion continues through 2027.
Use the coverage map at prodapt.com/coverage. 5G Ultra (mmWave) is available in
stadiums, airports, and dense urban zones in 45 cities.

Q: Does 5G use more battery?
A: 5G can use 10-20% more battery than LTE in weak signal areas because the phone
searches for optimal cells. Enable "Smart Data Mode" in settings to auto-switch to LTE
when 5G signal is weak.

Q: Is 5G safe?
A: Prodapt 5G operates within FCC exposure limits. Our towers comply with IEEE and
WHO guidelines for radio frequency emissions. Public health information at prodapt.com/5g-safety.

Q: Troubleshooting dropped 5G calls or data:
1. Toggle Airplane Mode for 10 seconds
2. Reset network settings (Settings > General > Reset > Reset Network Settings)
3. Update carrier settings when prompted
4. If issue persists at a specific location, report tower ID from Field Test Mode (*3001#12345#*)
   to support for tower-specific investigation.

Support: 1-800-5G-HELP | Live chat in Prodapt app

Prodapt - Billing Disputes and Adjustments Policy

1. DISPUTE WINDOW
Customers must report billing errors within 90 days of the statement date.
Disputes submitted after 90 days may be reviewed at Prodapt discretion but are
not guaranteed adjustment.

2. VALID DISPUTE CATEGORIES
Eligible for investigation and potential credit:
- Duplicate charges for the same service period
- Charges for services not activated or already cancelled
- Incorrect plan tier applied after documented plan change
- Roaming charges when Travel Pass was active (requires proof of activation timestamp)
- Equipment charges after verified return with RMA number
- Taxes calculated on incorrect address

NOT eligible for automatic credit:
- Usage overages when usage logs confirm consumption
- Late payment fees when payment was received after due date
- Promotional pricing expiration (customers notified 30 days prior)
- Third-party content purchases (app store, premium SMS)

3. INVESTIGATION TIMELINE
- Standard disputes: resolution within 10 business days
- Complex disputes involving partner carriers: up to 30 business days
- During investigation, disputed amount may be placed on hold (not sent to collections)

4. CREDIT LIMITS
Automatic credits without manager approval: up to $50 per incident
Supervisor approval required: $50.01 - $200
Director approval required: above $200

5. REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION
For roaming disputes: travel dates, destination, Travel Pass activation screenshot
For duplicate charges: statement showing both charges with transaction IDs
For cancellation disputes: cancellation confirmation number and date

6. PAYMENT DURING DISPUTE
Customers must pay undisputed portions of the bill by the due date to avoid late fees.
Disputed amounts on hold do not accrue late fees.

7. ESCALATION PATH
Tier 1 Billing Support -> Billing Resolution Team -> Ombudsman (for amounts >$500)

Contact Billing Resolution: 1-800-BILL-HELP or billing.disputes@prodapt.com

Prodapt - Device Upgrade and Trade-In Policy

ELIGIBILITY
Postpaid customers may upgrade devices when:
- 50% of device payment plan is completed, OR
- 24 months have passed since activation, OR
- Device is fully paid off

Business accounts follow contract-specific upgrade schedules (typically 24 or 36 months).

UPGRADE OPTIONS
1. Early Upgrade Program ($35 upgrade fee + return current device in good condition)
2. Buyout remaining device balance and purchase new device at full retail or installment
3. Trade-in credit applied to new device (see trade-in values below)

TRADE-IN VALUES (Sample - varies by condition)
iPhone 14 Pro (excellent): up to $450 credit
iPhone 13 (good): up to $280 credit
Samsung Galaxy S23 (excellent): up to $380 credit
Google Pixel 7 (good): up to $200 credit

Condition grades:
- Excellent: no cracks, fully functional, battery health >85%
- Good: minor scratches, fully functional, battery health >80%
- Fair: visible wear, functional with screen damage under 1 inch
- Poor: not eligible for trade-in credit (recycle program available)

NEW DEVICE INSTALLMENT
0% APR for 24 or 36 months on qualified credit.
Down payment may be required for customers with credit score below 650.

eSIM AND NUMBER TRANSFER
Upgrades preserve your phone number. eSIM profiles can be transferred via the Prodapt
app without visiting a store. Physical SIM swaps available free at retail locations.

RETURN POLICY
New devices may be returned within 14 days if unused and in original packaging.
Restocking fee of $50 applies after 14 days or if device shows usage.

INSURANCE
Prodapt Device Protection ($15/month) covers accidental damage, loss, and theft with
$99 deductible. Required for Early Upgrade Program participation.

Contact: upgrades@prodapt.com | Visit any Prodapt store with valid ID

Prodapt - Network Outage Response Procedures
Internal Reference Document (Customer-Facing Summary)

INCIDENT CLASSIFICATION
CRITICAL: Complete loss of service affecting >10,000 subscribers or entire metro region
HIGH: Significant degradation affecting 1,000-10,000 subscribers or multiple towers
MEDIUM: Single tower or sector outage affecting <1,000 subscribers
LOW: Performance degradation without complete service loss

INITIAL RESPONSE CHECKLIST
1. NOC validates alarm from monitoring system (SNMP traps, customer reports, social media)
2. Assign incident commander and open ticket in ServiceNow (INC prefix)
3. Identify affected towers by region, technology (LTE/5G), and estimated customer count
4. Notify regional field teams and activate backup power assessment for affected sites
5. Post initial status to status.prodapt.com within 30 minutes of confirmed outage

COMMON ROOT CAUSES
- Fiber backhaul cut (construction, vehicle accident): 35% of CRITICAL incidents
- Power outage at tower site without functioning generator: 25%
- RAN software bug after patch deployment: 15%
- Weather (lightning, ice loading on antennas): 15%
- Equipment hardware failure (RRU, baseband unit): 10%

CUSTOMER COMMUNICATION TEMPLATES
CRITICAL outage: SMS blast to affected ZIP codes within 1 hour
"We are aware of a service disruption in [REGION]. Our teams are working to restore
service. Updates at status.prodapt.com/inc/[INC_ID]"

RESTORATION VERIFICATION
Before closing incident:
- Run drive tests in affected area
- Confirm tower status = OPERATIONAL in NOC dashboard
- Verify customer complaint volume returns to baseline
- Document root cause and preventive actions in post-incident review

PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE
- Generator fuel level checks: monthly at all sites
- Battery backup tests: quarterly
- Software patch staging: 5% canary deployment before full rollout
- Fiber route inspections: semi-annual in high-risk construction zones

CUSTOMER IMPACT ESTIMATION
Use formula: affected_subscribers = sum(sector_capacity * outage_duration_hours)
for SLA credit calculations. Refer to sla_policy.txt for credit eligibility rules.

Prodapt - International Roaming Policy
Last Updated: March 2026

OVERVIEW
Prodapt offers international roaming in 180+ countries through partner networks.
Roaming charges and data speeds vary by destination zone and your home plan tier.

ROAMING ZONES
Zone A (Canada, Mexico): Included at no extra charge on Unlimited Plus and Business Pro plans.
  Data usage counts against your domestic allowance at 1:1 ratio.

Zone B (Western Europe, UK, Japan, South Korea, Australia):
  - Unlimited Plus: 10 GB/month included, then $10/GB
  - Standard Unlimited: $0.25/min voice, $0.10/SMS, $12/GB data
  - Business Pro: 25 GB/month included

Zone C (Latin America excluding Mexico, Eastern Europe, Middle East, Africa):
  - All plans: $0.35/min voice, $0.15/SMS, $15/GB data
  - Travel Pass available: $10/day for 500 MB high-speed data + unlimited low-speed

Zone D (Rest of World including remote islands):
  - Pay-as-you-go only: $1.50/min voice, $0.50/SMS, $20/GB data
  - Travel Pass not available in Zone D countries

TRAVEL PASS ACTIVATION
Enable Travel Pass before departure via:
- Prodapt mobile app > Settings > International Roaming
- SMS "TRAVEL ON" to 611
- Customer portal at my.prodapt.com

Travel Pass activates on first network registration abroad and charges one daily fee
per 24-hour period in the local time zone of the visited country.

DATA ROAMING SAFEGUARDS
All accounts have a default $100/month roaming spend cap. Customers may raise or lower
this cap in the app. When 80% of the cap is reached, SMS and email alerts are sent.

DEVICE COMPATIBILITY
Your device must support the frequency bands used in your destination. Prodapt
recommends checking band compatibility at prodapt.com/roaming-check before travel.
5G roaming is available in Zone A and B countries on select flagship devices only.

BILLING DISPUTES FOR ROAMING
Roaming charges appear on the bill cycle when usage occurred, not when you returned home.
Dispute roaming charges within 60 days by providing travel dates and destination.

Support: roaming@prodapt.com | While abroad dial +1-404-555-0199 (free from Prodapt SIM)

Prodapt - Service Level Agreement (SLA) Policy
Version 3.2 | Effective January 2026

1. NETWORK AVAILABILITY COMMITMENT
Prodapt guarantees 99.5% network uptime for postpaid business and enterprise customers
on our 4G LTE and 5G NR networks. Consumer prepaid plans are covered at 99.0% uptime.

2. OUTAGE CREDIT ELIGIBILITY
Customers may receive a service credit when a verified network outage exceeds:
- 4 consecutive hours for business plans (credit: one day of monthly service fee)
- 8 consecutive hours for consumer plans (credit: 50% of one day of service fee)
Credits are applied automatically within two billing cycles when the outage is logged in our
Network Operations Center (NOC) system with severity HIGH or CRITICAL.

3. MEAN TIME TO REPAIR (MTTR)
- CRITICAL outages (complete regional loss): target restoration within 4 hours
- HIGH severity (degraded service >50% customers in a cell sector): target 8 hours
- MEDIUM severity (localized tower issues): target 24 hours

4. EXCLUSIONS
SLA credits do not apply for outages caused by:
- Customer device failure or misconfiguration
- Scheduled maintenance announced 72 hours in advance
- Force majeure events (hurricanes, earthquakes, civil unrest)
- Third-party backhaul provider failures beyond Prodapt control

5. PERFORMANCE METRICS
Business customers may request quarterly reports showing:
- Average latency (target: <40ms on 5G, <60ms on LTE)
- Packet loss (target: <0.5%)
- Jitter (target: <15ms)

6. ESCALATION
If an outage exceeds MTTR targets, enterprise customers may escalate to their assigned
Customer Success Manager. Reference your account number and the NOC incident ID.

Contact: sla@prodapt.com | NOC Hotline: 1-800-NOC-HELP
